summary: Dark Star fireworks and soaring Good Lovin' dominate the set
blurb: The show shines with a smoldering Dark Star and Jerry’s electrifying lead on Good Lovin'. The Other One stretches out into free‑form improvisation before a tight Drums segment launches an excellent Turn On Your Lovelight. Overall the performance captures the historic, sunny vibe of the Polo Field free concert.
review: May 7 1969 at San Francisco’s Polo Field remains one of the most legendary early Dead outings – a sun‑drenched free show that still feels fresh after more than five decades. The band launches into an adventurous set that breaks the usual DS>SS>11>LL pattern, opening with Me And My Uncle and a loose Jam before diving deep into a scorching Dark Star that showcases Jerry’s petal‑steel work and his soaring vocal on Good Lovin'. The Other One erupts into a wild, free‑form excursion, feeding directly into a crisp Drums duo that propels the band into an inspired Turn On Your Lovelight. Across both soundboard recordings the performance is consistently praised for its adventurous flow, tight rhythm section, and Jerry’s standout leadership on Good Lovin', making this a must‑listen snapshot of the Dead in their early improvisational prime.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Both recordings express strong positive sentiment, repeatedly praising Dark Star, Good Lovin', The Other One and the overall historic vibe; no negative comments were found, just consistent acclaim for the performance.

best_recording.reason: Good‑quality soundboard capture that includes the full set with clear mix; while not as crisp as the other, it is the most complete and widely endorsed version.

key_highlights: Smoking Dark Star with extended improvisation
Jerry’s soaring vocal and lead on Good Lovin'
The Other One stretches into a wild free‑form jam
Tight Drums segment leading to an excellent Turn On Your Lovelight

band_performance.Jerry: Leads on Good Lovin' with petal steel, acoustic and electric guitar; his vocal is singled out as exceptional and drives the set’s energy.
band_performance.Bob: Sneaks a Weather Report Suite‑style guitar intro into the final seconds of Dark Star, adding an unexpected twist.
band_performance.Mickey: Provides a steady, swinging pulse throughout, especially during the extended Dark Star and the transition into Lovelight.
band_performance.Billy: Locks in with Mickey, delivering crisp fills that propel the jam forward and heighten the Lovelight climax.
